title: 10 best herbal essential oils for alzheimer’s disease
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for alzheimer’s disease are a blend of natural extracts derived from plants that help alleviate symptoms and potentially slow down the progression of this neurodegenerative disorder. These oils contain bioactive compounds that stimulate cognitive function, improve memory, and reduce inflammation. The best herbal essential oils for alzheimer’s disease include Ginkgo biloba, Bacopa monnieri, Centella asiatica, Curcuma longa, Rosmarinus officinalis, Zingiber officinale, Panax ginseng, Avena sativa, Melissa officinalis, and Salvia officinalis. If left untreated, alzheimer’s disease can significantly impact daily life, causing individuals to struggle with memory loss, communication, and mobility, ultimately affecting relationships, independence, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with Alzheimer's disease because they have been shown to significantly improve cognitive function, memory, and mood in individuals suffering from this devastating condition. The oil is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are common hallmarks of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, sage oil has been found to increase acetylcholine levels, a neurotransmitter involved in learning and memory, thereby enhancing cognitive performance and reducing symptoms of the disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and borneol, can reduce inflammation in the brain, which is a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease and may contribute to cognitive decline.

Antioxidant properties: Sage oil's antioxidant compounds, including rosmarinic acid and eucalyptol, can neutralize free radicals that damage brain cells and contribute to neurodegeneration, helping to slow down the progression of Alzheimer's.

Acetylcholinesterase inhibition: Sage oil's bioactive compounds, such as ursolic acid, have been shown to inhibit acetylcholinesterase, an enzyme that breaks down the neurotransmitter acetylcholine, which is important for memory and cognitive function in healthy individuals.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Barks: Sage barks contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve cognitive function and memory, making them a valuable component in essential oils for Alzheimer's disease treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves for optimal bioactive compound retention.
Steam distill the harvested leaves using a clevenger apparatus and water.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation process and separate it from water.
Filter the essential oil using a paper filter or coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the salvia officinalis essential oil in a cool, dark place to preserve its potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with Alzheimer's disease because its unique chemical composition is believed to have a positive impact on cognitive function and memory. The oil contains compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the brain, which is thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, the oil's antioxidants and flavonoids may help protect neurons from damage and promote healthy neural communication, ultimately supporting improved mental clarity and memory recall for individuals with this devastating condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Maidenhair Fern extract has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, potentially slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Amyloid-binding ability: Extracts from Maidenhair Fern have been found to bind to amyloid-β peptides, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, which may help prevent the accumulation of these toxic proteins in the brain.

Neuroprotective effects: The antioxidants and other compounds present in Maidenhair Fern extract may also provide neuroprotective effects, helping to protect neurons from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maidenhair Tree contain flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to possess neuroprotective properties that may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.

Barks: The barks of the tree have been used in traditional medicine to treat memory-related disorders, including Alzheimer's disease, due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried ginkgo biloba leaves in late autumn and store them in an airtight container for future use.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves using a food dehydrator at 50°c for 12 hours to preserve their bioactive compounds.
Combine one part dried ginkgo biloba leaves with two parts carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Infuse the mixture under low heat in a double boiler for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Filter the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container and store it in an airtight bottle at room temperature for up to one month.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with Alzheimer's disease because it has been shown to have a positive impact on cognitive function and memory. The oil contains compounds that help to improve focus, concentration, and mental clarity, making it easier for individuals suffering from the disease to recall memories and perform daily tasks. Additionally, lemon balm essential oils may also help to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common symptoms associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal lemon balm essential oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals in the brain, which can contribute to oxidative stress and neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's.

Cholinergic properties: Lemon balm essential oil contains rosmarinic acid, a compound that has been shown to increase acetylcholine levels in the brain, improving cognitive function and memory, which are often impaired in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.

Anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress, while also exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help combat free radicals and oxidative stress associated with Alzheimer's.
Leaves: High in rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant that has been shown to have neuroprotective effects and improve memory.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn and wash them gently with lukewarm water to remove impurities.
Dry the leaves in a warm dry place for 7-10 days or using desiccant until they reach a moisture content of 15%.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a steam distillation apparatus at a temperature of 100°c and pressure of 1 bar.
Collect the essential oil that separates from water in the condenser and filter it through a paper coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, keeping them away from direct sunlight and heat for future use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort essential oils helps with Alzheimer's disease because it has been traditionally used to improve cognitive function, memory, and mood. The oil contains compounds like hyperforin and hypericin that have been shown to inhibit the breakdown of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter important for memory formation and recall. By supporting the brain's natural ability to produce this neurotransmitter, St John's wort essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease such as memory loss, confusion, and difficulty concentrating. Additionally, its antioxidant properties may also help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St. John's Wort extract has antioxidant properties that help protect the brain from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The extract has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the brain, which is a common characteristic of Alzheimer's disease.

Serotonin regulation: St. John's Wort has been shown to regulate serotonin levels in the brain, which can help improve mood and cognitive function, potentially alleviating symptoms associated with depression and anxiety that are often comorbid with Alzheimer's.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of St John's Wort contain a high concentration of hyperforin, a compound shown to have neuroprotective properties that may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.

Roots: The roots of St John's Wort are rich in hypericin, a bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves from a pesticide-free area.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place for at least two weeks.
Steam distill one kilogram of dried st john's wort using 2 kilograms of water for 4 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a sterile glass bottle.
Store the final product in a dark glass vial with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Creeping thyme essential oils helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help combat the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with the condition. The oil's ability to cross the blood-brain barrier also allows it to target the affected areas directly, potentially improving memory and cognitive function. Additionally, its calming effects may help reduce anxiety and agitation, common symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Thyme essential oil's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage brain cells and contribute to cognitive decline associated with Alzheimer's.
Anti-inflammatory: Thyme oil's anti-inflammatory effects may help reduce inflammation in the brain, which is a common characteristic of Alzheimer's disease and can contribute to cognitive impairment.

Neuroprotective: Thyme essential oil contains compounds that have been shown to protect neurons from damage and death, potentially slowing down or halting the progression of neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain volatile compounds that have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing oxidative stress associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that has been found to have neuroprotective effects by scavenging free radicals and inhibiting beta-amyloid plaque formation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus serpyllum leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated, selecting healthy plants with vibrant colors.
Dry the plant material using a warm air dryer or dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours to preserve its natural properties.
Combine dried thymus serpyllum leaves and flowers with carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:10, steeping for 2-4 weeks in glass containers.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding solids and allowing the liquid to settle for 24 hours before transferring it to dark glass bottles.
Store the thymus serpyllum essential oil blend at room temperature, away from direct sunlight, for up to 6 months, shaking the bottle daily to ensure uniform distribution of active compounds.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi essential oils helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its natural ability to improve memory, concentration, and cognitive function. The oil contains a compound called saponins, which has been shown to enhance communication between brain cells, promoting a healthier brain environment. Additionally, Brahmi oil is rich in antioxidants, which help protect the brain from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing the risk of neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's. Regular use of Brahmi essential oils may help slow down cognitive decline and improve overall brain health.
remedy_properties: Cognitive Enhancing Properties: Brahmi essential oil has been shown to improve memory, concentration, and cognitive function by increasing blood flow to the brain and reducing inflammation, making it an effective natural treatment for improving mental clarity and focus.

Antioxidant Activity: Brahmi essential oil is rich in antioxidants that help protect the brain from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease, reducing the risk of cell death and oxidative stress.

Neuroprotective Properties: Brahmi essential oil contains compounds that have been shown to reduce the formation of amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, and may also help to prevent neurodegeneration by promoting the growth of new neurons and protecting against oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Brahmi contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, making them an ideal choice for extracting essential oils with potential neuroprotective and cognitive-enhancing properties.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, which are underground stems that store starch, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease when converted into essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried bacopa monnieri leaves in abundance at dawn on a sunny day weighing approximately 100 grams.
Combine the harvested leaves with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for two hours under low heat ensuring gentle and controlled infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids after one hour.
Store the final essential oil blend in dark-tinted glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort essential oils helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cognitive-enhancing properties. The oil has been shown to improve memory, learning, and mental clarity by promoting the growth of new neurons and neural connections in the brain. Additionally, it has been found to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which are common hallmarks of Alzheimer's disease. As a result, Asiatic pennywort essential oils may help alleviate symptoms such as forgetfulness, confusion, and mood changes associated with this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Asian Pennywort essential oil has antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are contributing factors to Alzheimer's disease.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce neuroinflammation, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, and protect against neuronal damage and loss.

Neuroprotective effects: Asian Pennywort essential oil has been shown to exhibit neuroprotective properties, which may help prevent or slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease by reducing amyloid-β aggregation and tau protein hyperphosphorylation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are valued for their potential to improve cognitive function and memory.
Rhyzomes: Containing a unique combination of alkaloids and saponins, rhyzomes may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with Alzheimer's.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centella asiatica leaves from a healthy plant with no signs of pests or diseases at dawn.
Wash the harvested leaves gently with distilled water and mild soap to remove dirt and contaminants thoroughly for 10 minutes.
Steam distill 100 grams of the washed leaves in 500 milliliters of distilled water at atmospheric pressure using a clevenger apparatus for 4 hours.
Separate the obtained essential oil from the hydrosol by allowing it to settle at room temperature for 24 hours without agitation or decantation.
Filter the clear top layer of essential oil through a sterile cotton cloth or paper filter into a dark glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator at 4 degrees celsius for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree essential oils helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its unique ability to stimulate the brain's neurotransmitters, specifically dopamine and serotonin. These neurotransmitters play a crucial role in regulating memory and cognitive function. The oil's ability to increase the production of these neurotransmitters can help to slow down or even reverse the progression of Alzheimer's disease, improving memory loss, confusion, and other symptoms associated with the condition.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in chasteberry oil may help reduce the inflammation and neurodegeneration associated with Alzheimer's disease, promoting a healthier brain environment.

Cognitive-enhancing activity: Chasteberry oil has been shown to improve cognitive function and memory by increasing neurotransmitter production and modulating the activity of important enzymes involved in learning and memory processes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chaste tree roots contain a high concentration of terpenes, which have been shown to have neuroprotective effects and may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vitex agnus castus leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated and before high temperatures.
Dry the harvested material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours to reduce moisture content.
Blend 20 grams of dried vitex agnus castus with 200 milliliters of vodka and allow it to steep for 14 days in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the tincture.
Store the tincture in an amber glass bottle away from light and heat for up to 2 years before using or repurposing it as an essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats essential oils helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain compounds that have been shown to have a positive impact on cognitive function. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in oat oil may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Saponins: Saponins in oats have been shown to have neuroprotective effects, possibly by reducing the formation of amyloid-beta plaques and tau tangles, which are characteristic of Alzheimer's disease.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh avena sativa leaves and dry them according to traditional methods within 24 hours of harvest.
Grind dried avena sativa leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for optimal extraction.
Combine 100 grams of ground avena sativa with 500 milliliters of high-quality carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Place the jar in a warm dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it daily, allowing for slow and steady infusion of active compounds into the carrier oil.
Filter the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids, and store the final essential oil in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with Alzheimer's disease because it has been shown to improve memory, cognitive function, and mood in individuals with mild cognitive impairment. The sedative properties of valerian oil can help reduce anxiety and stress, common symptoms associated with the disease. Neuroprotective properties: Valerian oil has been shown to inhibit the production of amyloid plaques and tau protein aggregates, common hallmarks of Alzheimer's disease, helping to preserve cognitive function.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots in late summer and dry them for two weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, approximately 100 grams per batch.
Combine the ground valeriana officinalis root powder with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for four hours at room temperature under direct sunlight, allowing the essential oils to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain and filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the valeriana officinalis essential oil.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat Alzheimer's disease with herbal essential oils is to combine specific blends in aromatherapy with conventional care. Lavender oil promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety, while peppermint oil improves memory recall. Rosemary oil enhances cognitive function and boosts mood. Bergamot oil alleviates stress and anxiety, while frankincense oil reduces inflammation. Blend these oils with a carrier oil and apply topically or inhale through steam inhalation to promote overall well-being and potentially slow disease progression.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to Alzheimer's disease that are treated with herbal essential oils are vascular dementia, Lewy body dementia, and frontotemporal dementia. Certain essential oils, such as lavender, rosemary, and frankincense, have been shown to improve cognitive function, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate symptoms of these dementias. Additionally, essential oils like peppermint and eucalyptus may also help with memory improvement and focus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its remarkable antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It enhances blood flow to the brain, improving memory and cognitive function. The oil's flavonoids and terpenoids work together to protect neurons from damage and promote the growth of new ones. This leads to improved concentration, memory recall, and overall mental clarity. Studies have shown that Ginkgo biloba essential oils may even slow down the progression of the disease, providing hope for those affected by Alzheimer's.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These constituents help by protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are key factors in the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Bilobalide: This constituent has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, potentially slowing the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Ginkgolides: These constituents have anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce blood viscosity, which may improve blood flow to the brain and reduce the risk of cognitive decline associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, the leaves are the primary source of ginkgo biloba essential oil, which is believed to help improve cognitive function and memory.
Buds: The buds contain a high concentration of bilobalide and ginkgolides, which are thought to have neuroprotective properties and may help slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are also rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are fully mature and dry.
Steam distill the harvested leaves to extract their essential oil using a distillation apparatus for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities immediately.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids in a cool dry place.
Age the essential oil for 2-3 weeks to allow its fragrance and therapeutic properties to mature completely.

Separate the essential oil from the distilled water through decantation and filter the oil to remove impurities.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it properly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng essential oils helps with Alzheimer's disease because it may improve cognitive function and memory by increasing blood flow to the brain and promoting the growth of new neurons. The ginsenosides present in Panax ginseng have been shown to have neuroprotective properties, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain. Additionally, it may enhance the production of neurotransmitters such as acetylcholine, which is often depleted in individuals with Alzheimer's disease, contributing to improved cognitive performance and memory.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These ginsenosides have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Panax ginseng essential oils have been shown to have neuroprotective effects, helping to prevent neuronal damage and death, and potentially slowing the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Phenolic acids: These phenolic acids possess antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the brain, potentially mitigating the symptoms and progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most used part due to their high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to have neuroprotective effects against Alzheimer's disease.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their content of volatile compounds, such as borneol and borneol acetate, which may help improve cognitive function and memory.
Stems: The stems are used due to their content of saponins, which may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help prevent the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 roots of panax ginseng, dried to 10% moisture content for 7 days, then sorted.
Steam distill the dried roots in a 1:3 ratio with water at 100°c for 4 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the herbal panax ginseng essential oil in a dark glass bottle at 5°c for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oils helps with Alzheimer's disease because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are associated with the progression of the disease. It may also improve sleep quality, cognitive function, and memory, as it contains sedative and anxiolytic properties that can promote relaxation and reduce stress. Additionally, Avena sativa essential oils may help to improve blood flow to the brain, potentially slowing down the decline in cognitive function.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as avenanthramides, have been shown to have neuroprotective effects and may help reduce beta-amyloid plaque formation and improve cognitive function.

Phenylethanoid Glycosides: Phenylethanoid glycosides have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help protect neurons from damage and promote neuroregeneration in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Avena sativa are commonly used to make essential oils for Alzheimer's disease due to their high concentration of fatty acids and antioxidants that may help improve cognitive function.
Leaves: The leaves of Avena sativa are used to make essential oils for Alzheimer's disease due to their content of flavonoids and phenolic acids that may have neuroprotective and antioxidant effects.

Buds: The buds of Avena sativa are used to make essential oils for Alzheimer's disease due to their high concentration of sesquiterpenes and other phytochemicals that may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh avena sativa roots and leaves for maximum potency and effectiveness.
Dry the harvested avena sativa material in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried avena sativa material at 100-110 degrees celsius for 3-4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation apparatus using a separatory funnel immediately.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cotton cloth to remove impurities completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its unique properties that promote cognitive function and memory improvement. The oil's high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate has been shown to have a positive impact on neuroprotection and anti-inflammatory responses, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain. This, in turn, may slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease and improve symptoms such as memory loss and confusion. The oil's calming and sedative effects also contribute to a better quality of life for individuals with Alzheimer's.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: This terpene has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, potentially reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help mitigate the progression of Alzheimer's disease by reducing oxidative damage and inflammation in the brain.

Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may contribute to its potential therapeutic effects in reducing the severity of Alzheimer's disease symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their high content of melissic acid, which has been shown to have a neuroprotective effect.
Stems: Stems are used to make essential oils rich in geraniol and linalool, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's.
Flowers: Melissa flowers are used to produce essential oils with a high concentration of linalool, which has been found to have a neuroprotective effect and may help improve cognitive function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature melissa officinalis leaves in the morning when the dew is still present on the leaves.
Steam distill the harvested melissa officinalis leaves in a distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a clean glass container and store it in a cool place.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities or contaminants present in the oil.
Label and store the final melissa officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils helps with alzheimer’s disease because of its neuroprotective properties, which aid in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain. The oil's high concentration of carnosic acid and ursolic acid has been shown to improve cognitive function and memory by inhibiting the production of beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of the disease. Additionally, its antioxidant properties may slow down the progression of the disease by protecting neurons from damage and promoting neural health. This leads to improved memory and cognitive function in individuals with Alzheimer's.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound may help with Alzheimer's disease by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are known to contribute to the progression of the disease.
Ursolic acid: This triterpenoid may help with Alzheimer's disease by reducing the formation of beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of the disease, and also by improving cognitive function and memory.
Carvacrol: This monoterpenoid may help with Alzheimer's disease by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, as well as by improving neuronal function and memory.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Salvia officinalis for making essential oils, as they contain high concentrations of bioactive compounds that have been shown to have neuroprotective effects.

Buds: Buds are another key component of Salvia officinalis essential oils, as they contain a high percentage of rosmarinic acid, a compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate Alzheimer's symptoms.

Stems: Stems of Salvia officinalis are also used for making essential oils, as they contain a high concentration of salvianolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help protect against neurodegeneration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves at dawn when moisture content is highest to ensure quality.
Steam distill 500g of fresh leaves with 2 liters of distilled water for 2 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate it from the condensed water using a separating funnel.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a dropper top and label it clearly.
Keep the essential oil in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.
